name: blood

Use this skill whenever the user asks to run the "Blood analysis agent", analyze a blood/bloodwork PDF or screenshot for the LEGO-piece pipeline, or generate provider/patient notes and 30-day/long-term plans from a blood report. NO internet access, no external medical knowledge, and no model prior knowledge may be used for clinical content — every clinical statement must trace back to a row in the bundled Excel sheet. If the user supplies an updated version of BloomCell_Bloodwork_Markers.xlsx, replace the file in references/ with the new one and always use the latest file present there. No script execution is used anywhere in this skill — every step is done directly (document reading, matching, ID/timestamp generation, validation) so it works in environments like NCAMEO that don't run scripts.

---

# blood

Generates the "Blood Analysis" LEGO piece for the NCAMEO multi-agent health-report pipeline. Takes one human-selected blood report file (PDF or screenshot) as input, and the bundled `references/BloomCell_Bloodwork_Markers.xlsx` sheet as the ONLY source of clinical rules, language, and ground truth. Produces exactly 8 JSON objects for human-in-the-loop review. Everything below is done directly by you — no scripts, no code execution.

## Hard rules — read first

1. **Zero internet / zero external knowledge.** Never call web_search, never fetch a URL, never fill in a marker's interpretation, severity, or recommendation from general medical knowledge. If a marker found in the report is NOT present in `references/BloomCell_Bloodwork_Markers.xlsx`, do not invent content for it — list it in a "not covered by ground truth sheet" note to the human instead of guessing (see Step 3).
2. **The Excel sheet is the only source of clinical language.** All `Explanation`, `Internal Notes`, `30-Day Plan`, and `Long-Term Plan` text in the output must be drawn from — or a faithful synthesis of — the matching row(s) in the sheet. Do not paraphrase in a way that changes clinical meaning; do not add recommendations, dosages, or claims that aren't grounded in that row.
3. **"Latest file always."** Before every run, check `references/` and use whichever `BloomCell_Bloodwork_Markers.xlsx` is there. If the user gives you an updated sheet, they'll overwrite the old one (same filename) — never fall back to a cached/older copy or to something you remember from an earlier run.
4. **Human-in-the-loop only.** This skill only produces the draft 8-object JSON for a human to review, edit, and approve. It never auto-submits or pushes the output anywhere.
5. **If the ground-truth sheet genuinely cannot be opened/read** (e.g. your environment can't render or open the `.xlsx` at all): do NOT proceed by guessing sheet content from this SKILL.md's own description, general medical knowledge, or anything resembling "domain knowledge as loaded." That defeats the whole point of this skill and risks ungrounded clinical content. Instead, stop and tell the human clearly: "The ground-truth sheet could not be opened in this environment — I cannot proceed without it." Let them decide how to proceed (e.g. provide the sheet content as pasted text instead).

## Workflow

### Step 1 — Load the ground truth
Open and read `references/BloomCell_Bloodwork_Markers.xlsx` directly (as a document/spreadsheet) to get every row: Marker Name, Internal Notes, Explanation, 30-Day Plan, Long-Term Plan. Note the exact marker names it contains — these are your match targets. Don't rely on memory of a previous run's sheet; always re-read the current file.

### Step 2 — Extract the report text
View the report directly (native document/vision reading) straight from its URL or file, whether it's a PDF or an image/screenshot. Transcribe every marker name + value + flag (H/L/normal) you see, verbatim, before doing anything else — never guess numbers from a glance.

### Step 3 — Match markers (verification loop)
For each marker name found in the report:
1. Try an exact (case-insensitive) match against the sheet's marker names.
2. If no exact match, try a normalized match (strip units/parentheses, common abbreviation aliases e.g. "A1c" ↔ "Hemoglobin A1c (%)", "Chol" ↔ "Cholesterol (mg/dL)").
3. Re-check the matched marker name actually appears in what you transcribed from the report in Step 2 before using it — don't let a plausible-sounding match slip through unverified.
4. Any report marker that cannot be confidently matched to a sheet row goes on a "not covered by ground truth sheet" list — do NOT generate clinical content for it.

### Step 4 — Decide what's notable
Only markers that are flagged abnormal in the report (H/L flags, out-of-range, or explicitly called out by the report) are candidates for detailed synthesis; use the sheet's "severity weight" language (embedded in the Internal Notes column text) to prioritize which findings drive the 4 summary points. Normal-range markers generally don't need individual mention unless the report itself highlights them.

### Step 5 — Build the 8 JSON objects
Produce **exactly 8 objects**, 2 per group, using the groups below in this exact order and exact string values.

| Group (exact string) | highlight object `text` | note object `text` |
|---|---|---|
| `Internal Notes` | Literal text copied exactly as-is from the report (the flagged value/marker line) — no interpretation added | Clinical-language synthesis drawn only from the matched rows' Internal Notes column content — doctor-facing |
| `Understanding Your Results` | Literal text copied exactly as-is from the report | Patient-friendly synthesis drawn only from the matched rows' Explanation column content |
| `Priority Actions - Next 30 Days` | Literal text copied exactly as-is from the report | Actionable synthesis drawn only from the matched rows' 30-Day Plan column content |
| `Long-Term Strategy` | Literal text copied exactly as-is from the report | Strategic synthesis drawn only from the matched rows' Long-Term Plan column content |

Every object must include:
- `id`: a unique GUID/UUID v4 that you generate yourself, format `xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-yxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx` (the `4` in the third group and `y` being one of 8/9/a/b are required for a valid v4). Generate 8 distinct ones — double-check none repeat before moving on.
- `date`: current timestamp, UTC ISO 8601, exactly `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`
- `type`: `"highlight"` or `"note"`
- `text`
- `group`: one of the 4 exact strings above
- `rationale`: 1–2 sentences — for highlight, brief context on why this line was pulled; for note, brief explanation of which sheet rows/logic produced this synthesis
- `referencesource`: exact source — for highlight text this is the report file name + page/line; for note text this is `"BloomCell_Bloodwork_Markers.xlsx - Bloodwork Markers sheet - Row N, Marker: <name>"` (list every row used if more than one marker feeds a note)

### Step 6 — Validate before showing the human
Before presenting the JSON, check it yourself against this list — go through each line explicitly, don't just skim:
- [ ] Exactly 8 objects total
- [ ] Exactly 2 objects per group (one highlight + one note), for all 4 groups, no more no less
- [ ] Every `id` is a plausible UUID v4 and all 8 are different from each other
- [ ] Every `date` matches `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ` exactly
- [ ] No `text`, `rationale`, or `referencesource` field is empty
- [ ] Every note's `referencesource` names a marker that is actually a row in the sheet you loaded in Step 1

If anything fails, fix it before presenting. Then show the JSON to the human along with the "not covered by ground truth sheet" list from Step 3 so they can see what was excluded and why.

## Output format reference

See `references/output_schema_example.json` for a fully-worked example of the exact shape expected.

## Notes on the "date" field naming clash

Two conventions for `date` were mentioned when this skill was defined: `MM/DD/YY HH:MM AM/PM` and UTC ISO 8601 (`2026-06-24T14:20:00Z`). This skill uses **UTC ISO 8601** — flag to the human if they actually wanted the other format.
